rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 18, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. DEFENDANT DEERE & COMPANY's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is continued to March 25, 2010 per ex parte order filed on March 12, 2010. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: DALE RIGG et al VS. ASTENJOHNSON, INC., 'F/K/A ASTEN INC et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 18, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 3. Defendant CNH America, LLC¿s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-3 and 7. The deposition testimony of Dr. Dichmann and the declaration of Mr. Lipari create a triable issue whether Mr. Rigg was permanently precluded from performing his regular occupation as a result of mesothelioma more than a year prior to the filing of the complaint. The objections to Mr. Lipari¿s declaration are overruled. The relevant statute of limitations for Ms. Rigg¿s loss of consortium claim is the two year personal injury statute. Plaintiff¿s deposition testimony creates a triable issue whether the materials removed and installed from and into defendant¿s combines were supplied by the defendant. Defendant¿s summary adjudication motion on issues 4-6 is moot as the causes of action covered by those issues have been dismissed. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOYCE JUELCH, ET AL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 18, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiffs¿ discovery responses are factually devoid with respect to asbestos content. Plaintiffs¿ evidence fails to create a triable issue whether the gaskets that defendant¿s employees worked with contained asbestos. The declaration of Charles Ay lacks proper foundation for his conclusion that the gasket material in question more likely than not contained asbestos. =(220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JOYCE JUELCH, ET AL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Thursday, March 18, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Metalclad Insulation Corporation¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Metalclad¿s alternate motion for summary adjudication is denied as to negligence (issue 1), strict liability (issue 2), and loss of consortium (issue 5) and moot as to the remaining issues. Metalclad has not established all the elements of a workers compensation exclusivity defense, as Mrs. Juelch¿s alleged take-home exposure to asbestos that Mr. Juelch brought home on his work clothes did not arise out of and in the course of Mrs. Juelch¿s employment with Metalclad. Metalclad has not established all the elements of a sophisticated user defense since there is no evidence that Mrs. Juelch received any training in the hazards of asbestos prior to the commencement of her laundering of Mr. Juelch¿s work clothes. Mr. Juelch has a loss of consortium claim because Mrs. Juelch has a valid personal injury cause of action against Metalclad. To the extent that Metalclad¿s motion is based on an exposure/product identification issue, Metalclad did not satisfy its initial burden on that issue. =(220/HEK)
